critic_raw:
The evaluation is framed as anomaly detection but the design treats it as standard supervised binary classification with a stratified random split. In genuine anomaly detection, defect examples are rare and often represent novel failure modes not seen during training. A stratified random split that ensures proportional defect representation across all splits does not reflect operational deployment, where new defect types will emerge over time. The model is being evaluated under conditions that are more favorable than production — it has seen proportional examples of every defect type during training.

Precision is chosen as the primary metric because stakeholders want to minimize false positives (unnecessary inspections). However, if the model is optimized and selected for precision, it can achieve arbitrarily high precision by narrowing its decision boundary to classify only the most obvious defects — at the cost of missing subtle, early-stage defects that are the highest-value targets. There is no described floor on recall or F1 during model selection, meaning the selected model may have near-useless recall.

The design notes 'stratification checks across time periods and inspectors' to address seasonal variation and inspection protocol changes, but this is a post-hoc diagnostic rather than a structural feature of the experimental design. If model selection and tuning are done without temporal stratification, the validation set performance will not reflect temporal distribution shift, and the post-hoc check is checking a model that was already selected under potentially biased conditions.

The 'traditional image processing techniques' baseline is described only generically. Without specifying whether this is a handcrafted feature + SVM pipeline, a threshold-based anomaly detector, or an unsupervised method, the comparison lacks reproducibility and the 'equal tuning effort' claim cannot be verified. The representativeness of the baseline matters enormously to whether the hypothesis is falsifiable.
all_issues_raised: A stratified random split ensures every defect type appears in training, but operational deployment faces novel defect types — the experiment evaluates the model under unrealistically favorable conditions that do not match the anomaly detection use case.

Optimizing model selection on precision alone without a recall floor allows the selected model to achieve high precision by becoming highly conservative, potentially missing the majority of actual defects in a way that would be unacceptable operationally.

Stratification checks across time periods are described as post-hoc diagnostics on an already-selected model, not as structural constraints on model selection — the validation performance that drove selection may already be contaminated by temporal distribution shift.
The 'traditional image processing techniques' baseline is underspecified, making the 'equal tuning effort' claim unverifiable and the comparison non-reproducible.
